active_answer: sailing to bondage with a detour via macrame
active_question: What sequence of rope uses does Rope365 invoke when explaining why rope vocabulary has such a large legacy?
reason: This tests a colorful sailing-to-macrame phrase rather than a rope term, definition, technique, resource, or safety concept.
support_evidence: Rope has been around for a while, from sailing to bondage with a detour via macrame, this creates a huge legacy on the vocabulary. The more foundational concepts tend to have acquired many names, and sometimes the same word can have different meanings from one community to another. For the sake of simplicity, we try to stick to one term per concept while mentioning alternate names. Which one was chosen is often a subjective choice and is a reflection of the period of time these texts were written. Language is fluid and continues to evolve. Check out the Vocabulary Guide to get deeper on this topic.
